items.criterion: `describeTimeline()` output includes a `Shot groups:` section listing each pinned shot group with resolved `shotName` (or `shotId` fallback), `shotId`, `trackId`, `clipIds`, and `mode` when `pinnedShotGroups` exist.
Each clip line in the timeline summary includes shot metadata when the clip belongs to a pinned shot group.
`SelectedClipPayload` and persisted `AgentTurn.attachments` accept optional shot id/name fields without breaking existing consumers.
The frontend selection send path (`useSelectedMediaClips` -> `AgentChat` -> `useSendMessage`) preserves `shot_id` and `shot_name` for timeline-selected clips.
Backend selected-clip normalization and continuation recovery preserve shot metadata across turns.
`buildSelectedClipsPrompt()` includes shot info when `shot_id` or `shot_name` is available.

Same-shot resolution is centralized and used by `runAgentLoop`, `executeCreateTask()`, `executeSetLora()`, and `executeDuplicateGeneration()`, preferring explicit shot metadata and falling back to generation lookup only when needed.
`executeDuplicateGeneration()` skips `findShotForGenerations()` when selected clips already provide a shared `shot_id`.
Clicking a shot-group label continues to select all clips in that group.
All existing tests in `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/` continue to pass.

New or updated unit tests cover timeline shot-group summaries, selected-clip shot metadata roundtrip, selected-clip prompt shot context, duplicate-generation's direct `shot_id` path, and the frontend send path.
The system prompt mentions shared-shot context guidance once structured shot metadata is available.
No new files exceed roughly 300 lines.
Manual smoke test: select a shot group, send an agent message, and verify shot context survives into a continuation turn.

unresolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the caller contract of `useSelectedMediaClips()` via [useTimelineEditorData](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSelectedMediaClips.ts#L29). This hook's caller currently provides `selectedClipIds` and `resolvedConfig`, not a resolved config object with `pinnedShotGroups`, so the plan still does not handle the actual arguments passed at that call site and needs to pull pinned groups from editor `data` instead.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Checked the caller contract of `useSelectedMediaClips()` via [useTimelineEditorData](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSelectedMediaClips.ts#L29). This hook's caller currently provides `selectedClipIds` and `resolvedConfig`, not a resolved config object with `pinnedShotGroups`, so the plan still does not handle the actual arguments passed at that call site and needs to pull pinned groups from editor `data` instead.

rationale: The plan is ready to execute. The score improved from 5.0 to 7.5, preflight is clean, and the original major blockers were addressed. The five remaining significant flags all collapse to one narrow issue: step 3 names the wrong frontend source for pinned groups. That is a real critique, but it is a localized wording error, not a structural gap. The repo already exposes `data: TimelineData | null` alongside `resolvedConfig` through `useTimelineEditorData()` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types.ts` and `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/TimelineEditorContext.tsx`, so execution can pull shot membership from `data?.config.pinnedShotGroups` without another planning loop.
signals_assessment: Weighted score is moving in the right direction from 5.0 to 7.5 on iteration 2, preflight remains fully green, and there are no recurring critiques or churn signals. The remaining open flags are all variants of the same frontend source-path mismatch around `useSelectedMediaClips()`, not independent architectural blockers, so the continuation decision should favor execution rather than another plan revision.
warnings: Do not widen `ResolvedTimelineConfig` just to carry pinned groups unless execution uncovers a separate need; the editor context already exposes raw `data.config`.
Keep the existing shot-group click-selection path unchanged and use its current test as the regression guardrail.

settled_decisions.decision: Frontend shot membership resolution will read from editor `data.config.pinnedShotGroups`, not from `ResolvedTimelineConfig`.
settled_decisions.rationale: That is the real repo surface exposed through `useTimelineEditorData()`.

settled_decisions.decision: Existing single-click shot-group selection stays as-is.
settled_decisions.rationale: `TimelineCanvas` already selects group clips on label click and already has regression coverage.

settled_decisions.decision: Same-shot resolution remains a shared backend helper that prefers explicit selected-clip shot metadata before generation-based fallback.

orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.

signals.idea: Implement first-class shot selection and structured timeline context for the video editor agent: single-click shot-group selection, agent payloads with shot and clip timeline metadata, reliable same-shot semantics, and a duplicate/edit/place-after-selected workflow with tests.

resolved_flags.concern: Timeline summary context: the plan assumes backend timeline config already carries shot names, but `pinnedShotGroups` only stores `shotId`/`trackId`/`clipIds`/`mode`, so `describeTimeline()` cannot emit shot names without an additional shot lookup or a widened input contract.
resolved_flags.resolution: FLAG-001 through FLAG-003 indicate the original plan was aimed at the wrong root cause in one area: the UI click-to-select affordance already exists, so adding a new `TimelineCanvas` callback would duplicate working code instead of fixing the missing shot-context pipeline. The revised plan removes that stale frontend scope, treats shot names as external data that must be loaded explicitly on the backend, and centralizes same-shot resolution so `runAgentLoop`, `create_task`, `set_lora`, and `duplicate_generation` all use the same shot-context rules rather than patching only `duplicate_generation`.

resolved_flags.concern: Selected-clip payload plumbing: Recurring debt: the plan updates some payload types but misses the stored attachment path, so `shot_id`/`shot_name` would be dropped by AgentChat send mapping and by attachment persistence/recovery across turns.
resolved_flags.resolution: FLAG-001 through FLAG-003 indicate the original plan was aimed at the wrong root cause in one area: the UI click-to-select affordance already exists, so adding a new `TimelineCanvas` callback would duplicate working code instead of fixing the missing shot-context pipeline. The revised plan removes that stale frontend scope, treats shot names as external data that must be loaded explicitly on the backend, and centralizes same-shot resolution so `runAgentLoop`, `create_task`, `set_lora`, and `duplicate_generation` all use the same shot-context rules rather than patching only `duplicate_generation`.

resolved_flags.concern: Same-shot semantics: the plan only applies direct `shot_id` to `duplicate_generation`, but shot resolution for travel defaults, active references, task shot reuse, and video-travel LoRAs still goes through `findShotForGenerations()`, so the feature remains only partially first-class.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 2. Weighted score trajectory: 5.0 -> 7.5. Plan deltas: 88.5%. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 3. Open significant flags: 5.

flag_resolutions.evidence: `useSelectedMediaClips()` currently reads from `useTimelineEditorData()` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSelectedMediaClips.ts`, and the context type exposes both `data: TimelineData | null` and `resolvedConfig` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types.ts`. `TimelineData` is therefore already available to the hook through `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/TimelineEditorContext.tsx`.
flag_resolutions.rationale: The critique is correct, but it points to a small source-path correction during execution rather than a need for another planning pass. The implementation should use `data?.config.pinnedShotGroups` as the pinned-group source.

flag_resolutions.evidence: `ResolvedTimelineConfig` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts` does not include `pinnedShotGroups`, while the editor data context separately carries raw `data` with timeline config in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types.ts`.
flag_resolutions.rationale: This is the same narrow mismatch as the correctness flag. The plan intent is still sound; execution just needs to use the raw timeline config surface that already exists in context.

flag_resolutions.evidence: The caller contract for `useSelectedMediaClips()` comes from `useTimelineEditorData()` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/TimelineEditorContext.tsx`, whose data context includes `data` and `resolvedConfig` separately in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types.ts`.
flag_resolutions.rationale: The actual caller shape is known and sufficient. This does not justify another loop; it simply settles which existing argument source the executor must use.
